---
title: Rural business entry rates declined across all regions since 1980
description: Tracks the share of establishments entering the market in the past 12
  months for rural counties, by Census region, from 1980 to 2023.
date: '2026-05-07'
layout: default
permalink: /charts/rural-economic-outlook-4-1-rural-region-entry-change-since-1980-lc/
categories: []
tags: []
image: 4.1_rural_region_entry_change_since_1980_lc.svg
chart:
  title: Rural business entry rates declined across all regions since 1980
  chartType: line
  interactive: false
  dataSource: CORI analysis
  sourceUrl: https://github.com/ruralinnovation/data-rural-economic-outlook
  featured: false
producedBy: rural-economic-outlook
usesDatasets:
  - census-bds
usesPackages:
  - cori-data-bds
usesResources: []
format:
  gfm:
    toc: false
    wrap: none
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

![Plots establishment entry rates for rural counties in each Census region since 1980, revealing a long-run structural decline in rural business dynamism that predates the Great Recession.](./4.1_rural_region_entry_change_since_1980_lc.svg)

## Overview

Plots establishment entry rates for rural counties in each Census region since 1980, revealing a long-run structural decline in rural business dynamism that predates the Great Recession.

## Key Findings

- Entry rates have fallen in all four Census regions, suggesting a secular — not cyclical — trend.
- The South shows the highest rural entry rates throughout the series; the Northeast the lowest.
- Rates compressed across regions over time, narrowing regional differences in business creation.
- The post-2020 uptick across regions may reflect pandemic-era entrepreneurship but has not reversed the long-run trend.

## Reproducibility

Generated by `R/viz/presentation/biz_entry_region_lc.R` in the producing project.
